test.name: A low score must not dismiss a strong qualitative match
test.description: Link assertion a_003 — an 1850 census record indexed under the transcription-variant surname 'Flinn'. same_person returns a low score (0.32) because the variant name tanks the name component, but correlation analysis is strong: age 5 in 1850, Schuylkill County, in the household of a Thomas Flinn — matching the subject on every identifier except spelling. person-evidence must still create the link; the low score must not dismiss it.

input.user_message: Link assertion a_003 — the 1850 census record for 'Patrick Flinn' — to the right person in the tree.

judge_context: same_person returns a low 0.32 because 'Flinn' is a transcription variant, but correlation is strong — age, year, county, and the father's name all match the subject
person-evidence must still create a person_evidence entry for a_003 — the low score is an input, not a veto; correlation analysis carries the decision
The rationale should explain that 'Flinn' is a likely transcription variant of 'Flynn' and that the non-name identifiers all corroborate the match
